steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Insert the graph information
steps.content: The slope is $5$, so $m=5$. The graph passes through $(0,-7)$, so the $y$-intercept is $-7$ and $b=-7$.
